# docs(agentos): MemoryCore.md — extract operational/tooling reference to tooling docs; rewrite to compelling conceptual guide (bar)

Part of #14310. Refs #14310. Completes the quality bar that the de-stale pass (merged #14334, Resolves #14317) did not reach.

**Problem.** #14334 fixed the *facts* but left `learn/agentos/MemoryCore.md` a **355-line spec-dump**: roughly half is internal tooling/reference — the full MCP Tools catalog, the Healthcheck Response Shape payload spec (including a migration-census **changelog** note), the Backup/Restore CLI flag tables (incl. `--force-topology-mismatch`), the Configuration file format, and runtime-primitive internals. Operator (2026-06-29): *"does NOT meet my quality bar, not even close… internal tooling crap… not compelling to anyone."* (My review approving #14334 missed this — I verified the opening, not the whole guide.)

The guide **conflates two doc types**: (1) the *conceptual* "what Memory Core is + why it's the moat" guide (the bar: compelling narrative, dual-audience), and (2) *operational tooling reference*. The latter belongs in the dedicated tooling docs that already exist.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] **Extract** the operational/reference sections to their existing homes — `tooling/RestorationRunbook.md` (backup/restore CLI + flags + safeguards), `tooling/MemoryCoreMcpApi.md` (the MCP tool catalog), `tooling/MultiTenantMigrationGuide.md` (migration census) — **verifying each target already covers the content before deleting** (no unique-info loss). Replace the inline dumps with a tight "Operations & reference" link section.
- [ ] **Cut** pure changelog / healthcheck-payload spec that is not conceptual (relocate any genuinely-useful bit).
- [ ] **Rewrite** what remains as a compelling end-to-end narrative arc (problem → why → how → what's in it for you), keeping the restored hippocampus / stigmergy / dual-audience spine. A guide that earns the read, not a reference manual.
- [ ] Every diagram + cross-link **render-verified**.

**⚠️ V-B-A grounding:** the merged `MemoryCore.md` (origin/dev) + the three tooling docs (confirm coverage) + the #14310 content rubric.

**Files:** `learn/agentos/MemoryCore.md` (+ the tooling docs if content must move, not just link).
